Dear Mr. Miller: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our No. A-84-252 
I am in receipt of a copy of your letter to Roger Brown, 
Director of our Enforcement Division. 
In that letter you 
request that your pending advice request be treated as a formal 
complaint by that Division. 
Therefore, I am transmitting to 
Mr. Brown the materials which you forwarded with your advice 
request. 
To clarify my telephone conversations with you, we are 
unable to provide specific advice under Government Code Section 
83114 to your organization in this instance because Common Cause 
had no "duty or obligation" under the Political Reform Act. 
However, we were preparing a responsive letter which would have 
discussed the requirements of the law in general terms. 
You 
prefer a more focused response to the specific facts involved 
and have, instead, elected to request an enforcement 
investigation. 
That is your prerogative. 
If we can be of further assistance in the future, please do 
not hesitate to calIon us.

By LARRY HICKS

SAN BERNARDINO -
Coun-
cilman Ralph Hernandez, the only 
elected official here opposing thl' 
city's attempt to annex Highland, 
has accused the Sail Bernardino 
Area Chamber of Commerce of 
misusing a $56,000 city grant to 
survey attitudes of Highland resi-
dents toward annexation. 
City Attorney Ralph H. Prince 
and a consultant hired to conduct 
the survey sald Wednesday th,' 
money has not been misused nor 
has any provision in the 
agreement between the city and 
the chamber been violated_ 
"I have gone to great lengths to 
ensure that the integrity of this 
(survey) is maintained," said John 
Husing, the chamber consultant 
conducting the survey. 
Survey interviewers are in-

distributing the survey question-
naires to Highland residents, Hus 
ing said, In facL the interviewers 
also are Highland residents who 
survey the neighborhoods where 
they live, he said. 
But Hernandez, who opposed

the study is tainted by the poli-
ticking of a Busing employee, 
Reggie Greene, a member of a 
black men's organization that re-
cently endorse annexation. 
Greene is a member of the 
Westside Action Group, which last

nandez said two members of 
WAG, whose names he would not 
r('veal. told him Greene spoke in 
favor of annexation and the en-
dorsement.

zier, who supports annexation

SAN BERNARDINO AREA CHAMBER OF COMMERCE SURVEY 
The purpose of this interview is to provide you with facts about 
the impact on your home and neighborhood of joining the City of 
San Bernardino. 
This interview is divided into topics. 
At the 
end of each topic I will ask your if you have further questions. 
If you do, I 
w~ll try to answer them, or get answers for you. 
TOPIC: TAXES

will cost.

Your letter requesting advice under the Political 
Reform Act has been referred to Robert E. Leidigh, an 
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at 
(916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex 
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to 
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21 
working days.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether costs for literature distributed by Common Cause regarding a San Bernardino annexation campaign, partially funded by the City of San Bernardino, constitute a reportable expenditure under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC was unable to provide specific advice under Government Code Section 83114 because Common Cause had no 'duty or obligation' under the Political Reform Act. The agency was preparing a general discussion of legal requirements but the requester elected to pursue an enforcement investigation instead.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple components: formal advice letter from FPPC, internal memorandum, campaign literature, news articles, and survey materials. The core FPPC response is brief and indicates declination of specific advice with referral to enforcement. No formal Q/C sections with verbatim text found in main advice letter. The annexation context and literature examples are background materials attached to the request.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether costs for literature distributed by Common Cause regarding a San Bernardino annexation campaign, partially funded by the City of San Bernardino, constitute a reportable expenditure under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC was unable to provide specific advice under Government Code Section 83114 because Common Cause had no 'duty or obligation' under the Political Reform Act. The agency was preparing a general discussion of legal requirements but the requester elected to pursue an enforcement investigation instead.

Hobart Blvd., Suite 226 Los Angeles, CA 90005 Dear Mr. Miller: Re: Your Request for Advice Our No. A-84-252 I am in receipt of a copy of your letter to Roger Brown, Director of our Enforcement Division. In that letter you request that your pending advice request be treated as a formal complaint by that Division. Therefore, I am transmitting to Mr. Brown the materials which you forwarded with your advice request. To clarify my telephone conversations with you, we are unable to provide specific advice under Government Code Section 83114 to your organization in this instance because Common Cause had no "duty or obligation" under the Political Reform Act. However, we were preparing a responsive letter which would have discussed the requirements of the law in general terms. You prefer a more focused response to the specific facts involved and have, instead, elected to request an enforcement investigation. That is your prerogative. If we can be of further assistance in the future, please do not hesitate to calIon us. REL:plh cc: Roger Brown W. R. embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter (A-84-252) addresses Steven Miller's request regarding whether campaign literature costs constitute expenditures, with partial City of San Bernardino funding. The FPPC declines to provide specific advice but notes the matter has been referred to the Enforcement Division per Miller's request.
